subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in literature.
eligibleContentText: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences and/or generalizations drawn from the text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in literature.
eligibleContentText: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through relevant details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in literature.
eligibleContentText: Describe how the plot of a particular story, drama, or poem unfolds, as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of craft and structure in literature.
eligibleContentText: Determine an author's purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text; explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text; describe the effectiveness of the point of view used by the author.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of craft and structure in literature.
eligibleContentText: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of craft and structure in literature.
eligibleContentText: Determine how the author uses the meaning of words or phrases, including figurative and connotative meanings, in a text; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of connections within, between, and/or among texts.
eligibleContentText: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories, dramas, poems, historical novels, fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of vocabulary and figurative language in literature.
eligibleContentText: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies (context clues such as a word's position or function; common Greek or Latin affixes and roots).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of vocabulary and figurative language in literature.
eligibleContentText: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings (simile, metaphor, personification, hyperbole; word relationships such as cause/effect, part/whole, item/category, synonym/antonym; connotations vs. denotations).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 6 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content
